DESCRIPTION: In the Kingdom of Wyverngarde, the average sentence for vehicular manslaughter is two years of imprisonment. There is no fine.
"Died at 1:12 AM after being stabbed from behind with a magical blade. Fatal wound shows heavy traces of transmutation magic."
Celeste McCoy’s Spell Compendium. It contains the formula for only one spell: Mage Blade.
Garrick Pierce's Spell Compendium. It contains the formulas for the following spells: Prestidigitation, Conjure Light, Conjure Water, Frost Wave, and Shape Liquid.
Celeste is a mercenary by trade. It's a little unexpected, but who am I to judge?
Celeste McCoy learned magic so she could support her family. Apparently, many people will pay extra for a mercenary that can use magic.
The alleged murder weapon. It's an ornately designed sword, but it has no magical properties.
The alleged murder weapon. It's an ornately designed sword, but it has no magical properties. Traces of transmutation magic were detected on it.
A picture of Flinhart McCoy after he was murdered. It shows the traces of transmutation magic that were on the sword.
A geometrically scaled map of the Crime Scene.
A geometrically scaled map of the Crime Scene. Flinhart died at the location of the V marker. This was also the location of where the murder must have occurred.

A geometrically scaled map of the Crime Scene. Flinhart died at the location of the V marker. Garrick Pierce claims to have witnessed the murder from the W marker. But that's impossible, as he wouldn’t have had proper lighting.
The McCoy Inn is outfitted with several lamps powered by artificery. However, the arcane power cell has been broken for weeks and the lamps can’t be turned on.

The McCoy Inn is outfitted with several lamps powered by artificery. However, the arcane power cell has been broken for weeks and the lamps can’t be turned on. This means that the lights were not operational during the murder.
The shattered remains of a glass bottle. You found it next to Flinhart McCoy’s body. The label says the words: "Lotus Juice".
The shattered remains of a glass bottle. Flinhart McCoy was holding this bottle and dropped it during the murder. The label says the words: "Lotus Juice".

The shattered remains of a glass bottle. He was holding this bottle and dropped it during the murder. It contained Celeste's favorite drink: Lotus Juice. Flinhart McCoy retrieved it from his room after his argument with Celeste.
The key to Celeste McCoy's bedroom in the McCoy Tavern.
Flinhart McCoy’s sister died during an accident involving the local lord and his magic. Ever since then, Flinhart has hated magic and the mages who wield them.
